HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted. Defendant Dentsply International, Inc.'s motion to compel depositions and production of documents is denied. If defendant had brought a motion for leave to take a subsequent deposition of plaintiffs Nathan and Weinstat, the Court would not find good cause within the meaning of CCP Section 2025.610 after reviewing the first, second, and third amended complaint and the memoranda submitted by the parties. Off Calendar Oct-03-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Mattew S. Gabe for plaintiff and moving party: Marvin C. Weinstadt
TELEPHONIC APPEARANCE by Maria C. Roberts for defendant and opposing party: Detsply International Inc.

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to deem certain matters admitted as to request for admission #51:
The motion is denied. However, the Court orders that the following phrase is stricken as non-responsive, lacking foundation and irrelevant: 'a fact of which the FDA has been aware since Cavitron was first approved by the FDA more than 40 years ago.' In addition, defendant shall answer the following interrogatories ordered by the Court within 15 days:

1) When did defendant represent to the FDA that Cavitron is a non-sterile water delivery system.
2) Identify all documents that refer to or relate to defendant representing to the FDA that Cavitron is a non-sterile water delivery system.
3) Identify all witnesses that have knowledgeable of defendant representing to the FDA that Cavitron is a non-sterile water delivery system. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

RULING: Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF MARVIN WEINSTADT, RICHARD NATHAN MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Second Amended Complaint To Add Plaintiffs & Conform To Proof GRANT IN PART. PLTF MAY AMEND THE AC TO CLARIFY ALLEGATIONS RE THE CAVITRON AND SURGERY. PLTF MAY AMEND TO (1) CLARIFY THE ALLEGATIONS RE BIOFILM; (2) ADD A SUBCLASS AND INCLUDE ALLEGATIONS RE DEFT'S DIFFERENT DIRECTIONS W/RE TO SURGERY; (3) ADD DR. HUMENIUK AS A CLASS REPRESENTATIVE. Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admission, Set One, Form Interrogatory No.17.1 And For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-05-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Paul Nelson for plaintiff and moving party
Maria C. Roberts for defendant and opposing party
HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: 1) plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to request for production set #1:
Granted in part and denied in part. The motion is timely pursuant to CCP Section 1005 and 2031.
#21: Denied. The objections are overruled and stricken. The response is otherwise sufficient.
#25: Granted. The objections are overruled and stricken. Defendant is ordered to respond without limiting the search for responsive documents to service or maintenance logs relating to service calls necessitated by the failure to follow recommended protocols for the use and care of the Cavitron. If defendant has produced all records of service or maintenance, that representation shall be verified.

Pursuant to Pioneer Electronics Inc. v. Superior Court, the parties shall by agreement designate an independent neutral entity that will send a letter to persons who have had their names and addresses redacted from the records produced in responses to request #25 notifying them of the pending litigation. The parties are ordered to meet and confer as to the text of the letter. Defendant is ordered to provide the names and addresses of the third party dentists to the designated entity. All responses are to be sent to the entity who will then provide the parties with the responses of the persons who expressly consent to the disclosure of their name and address to counsel for plaintiff. The cost of retaining the independent entity shall be the responsibility of plaintiff.
All requests for monetary sanctions are denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
